evidence: The Océan class ironclads were a group of three wooden-hulled armored frigates built for the French Navy in the mid to late 1860s.
The ships were designed by Henri Dupuy de Lôme as an improved version of the Provence-class ironclads.
These ships had their main armament mounted in four barbettes on the upper deck.
The original armament consisted of four 240-millimeter guns in the barbettes.
The Océan-class ships had one horizontal return connecting rod compound steam engine driving a single propeller.
They carried 650 metric tons of coal which allowed them to steam for approximately 3,000 nautical miles.
Marengo was a wooden-hulled, armored frigate of the Océan class, built for the French Navy in the mid to late 1860s.
She participated in the French occupation of Tunisia in 1881.
The Océan-class ironclads were designed by Henri Dupuy de Lôme as an improved version of the Provence-class ironclads.
Marengo had a maximum draft of 9.09 meters and displaced 7,749 metric tons.
Marengo's armament was upgraded to four 274-millimeter guns in the barbettes.
By 1885 two more 274-millimeter guns had been added.
Océan was a wooden-hulled, armored frigate built for the French Navy in the mid to late 1860s and the lead ship of her class.
She was commissioned in 1870 to participate in the Franco-Prussian War.
The ships were central battery ironclads with the armament concentrated amidships.
Océan had a maximum draft of 9.09 meters and displaced 7,749 metric tons.
The Océan-class ships had one horizontal return connecting rod compound steam engine driving a single propeller.
On sea trials the engine produced 3,600 indicated horsepower.
Tifon was an Uragan-class monitor built for the Imperial Russian Navy in the mid-1860s.
She was only active when the Gulf of Finland was not frozen.
Tifon was 201 feet long overall, with a beam of 46 feet and a draft of 10.16 – 10.84 feet.
The ship was fitted with a two-cylinder, horizontal direct-acting steam engine.
Tifon was designed to be armed with a pair of 9-inch smoothbore muzzle-loading guns.
The Rodmans were replaced around 1876 with the originally intended nine-inch rifled guns.
The French ironclad Thétis was a wooden-hulled armored corvette built for the French Navy in the late 1860s.
During the Franco-Prussian War she was assigned to a squadron of French ships.
Thétis measured 69.03 meters between perpendiculars, with a beam of 14.13 meters.
Thétis mounted her four 194-millimeter Modèle 1864 breech-loading guns in the central battery.
The ship had a single horizontal return connecting-rod steam engine driving a single propeller.
Her engine was powered by four oval boilers.

evidence: The 20th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the New Zealand Military Forces
It participated in the Battles of Greece and later in Crete.
It suffered heavy losses during Operation Crusader
The regiment participated in the Italian Campaign, fighting in actions at Orsogna and later at Cassino.
The 2nd Parachute Brigade was an airborne forces brigade formed by the British Army during the Second World War
It was initially part of the 1st Airborne Division but in 1943... became an independent formation.
The brigade saw active service in Italy, the South of France and Greece
At the end of the European war the brigade returned to the United Kingdom.
The 26th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army
It was assigned to the 7th Brigade and consisted of personnel recruited from the states of Queensland and Tasmania.
The entire 26th Battalion... was committed to the Bougainville campaign
They saw action against the Japanese from late 1944 until the end of hostilities in August 1945.
The 23rd Battalion was raised in Victoria in March 1915 as part of the formation of the 2nd Division of the Australian Imperial Force
It was sent to Egypt to complete its training.
The battalion was sent to Egypt to complete its training, before being committed to the Gallipoli Campaign
They remained on the peninsula until the evacuation of Allied troops in December.
The 2/6th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army that served during the Second World War
The battalion formed part of the 6th Division and was among the first troops raised by Australia during the war.
The battalion was withdrawn from the Middle East to help face the threat posed by the Japanese in the Pacific
They deployed to New Guinea in January 1943, fighting around Wau.

evidence: The Australian weasel shark (Hemigaleus australiensis) is an uncommon species of ground shark in the family Hemigaleidae.
It is viviparous, with the developing embryos nourished through a placental connection and born after a six-month gestation period.
Feeding almost exclusively on octopuses and other cephalopods, the Australian weasel shark mostly hunts near the sea floor.
Thus, it has been listed as Least Concern by the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN).
The sicklefin weasel shark (Hemigaleus microstoma) is an uncommon species of ground shark in the family Hemigaleidae.
It is viviparous, in which the unborn young form a placental connection to their mother.
The sicklefin weasel shark is a specialist predator of cephalopods.
Given that fishing activity is intense throughout its range, the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) has assessed this species as Vulnerable.
The banded houndshark (Triakis scyllium) is a species of houndshark, in the family Triakidae, common in the northwestern Pacific Ocean.
This shark reaches 1.5 m (4.9 ft) in length.
Nocturnal and largely solitary, the banded houndshark preys on benthic invertebrates and bony fishes.
Because fishing does not appear to have diminished this shark's population, the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) has listed it under Least Concern.
The Izak catshark (Holohalaelurus regani) is a species of catshark, belonging to the family Scyliorhinidae, common off the coasts of South Africa and southern Namibia.
This species reaches 69 cm (27 in) in length.
Bottom-dwelling and perhaps relatively active in nature, the Izak catshark feeds mainly on a diverse array of bony fishes, crustaceans, and cephalopods.
Despite the fishing pressure, its population has been increasing and thus the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) has assessed it as Least Concern.
The hardnose shark (Carcharhinus macloti) is a species of requiem shark, in the family Carcharhinidae.
A small bronze-coloured shark reaching a length of 1.1 m (3.6 ft).
Common and gregarious, the hardnose shark is a predator of bony fishes, cephalopods, and crustaceans.
Given its low reproductive rate, the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) has listed it as Near Threatened.

common_topic.description: The documents provide detailed descriptions of various shark species, their feeding habits, reproductive modes, and conservation statuses.

evidence: The 2011 – 12 Princeton Tigers men 's basketball team represented Princeton University during the 2011 – 12 NCAA Division I men 's basketball season.
They finished the season 20 – 12, 10 – 4 in Ivy League play to finish in third place.
The team got off to slow starts. It started the season with a 1 – 5 record, but won 18 of its final 24 games.

The 2012 Alabama Crimson Tide football team represented the University of Alabama in the 2012 National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA) Division I Football Bowl Subdivision (FBS) football season.
They finished the season with a record of 13 wins and 1 loss (13 – 1 overall, 7 – 1 in the SEC).
Alabama qualified for the 2013 BCS National Championship Game against Notre Dame, where they won 42 – 14 and captured the 2012 national championship.
The 2011 LSU vs. Alabama football game was a regular-season college football game between the unbeaten LSU Tigers and the unbeaten Alabama Crimson Tide.
Coined a 'Game of the Century,' this was the first time two Southeastern Conference (SEC) football teams came into a regular season matchup undefeated.
In a game dominated by defense and special teams, LSU won in overtime 9 – 6.
Alabama missed a 52-yard field goal in overtime.
The 2007 Navy vs. North Texas football game was a regular-season college football game between the Navy Midshipmen and the North Texas Mean Green.
The game holds the record for the most combined points scored in a National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA) Division I Football Bowl Subdivision (FBS) regulation game with 136 total points.
With the win the Midshipmen improved to 6 – 4, making the team bowl-eligible for the fifth straight year.
The 2010 Alabama Crimson Tide football team represented the University of Alabama in the 2010 NCAA Division I FBS football season.
They finished the season with a record of ten wins and three losses (10 – 3, 5 – 3 in the SEC).
Alabama entered the season as defending national champions, and began the 2010 season as the preseason number one team in both the AP and Coaches' Polls.

In combat, the player's active party members will consist of only first three members of their.

The game centers on the quest of Mario and Luigi to save Dinosaur Land from Bowser.
It built on the gameplay of previous Mario games, introducing new power-ups that augment character abilities.
Naruto : Clash of Ninja is a 3-D cel-shaded fighting game developed by Eighting and published by D3 Publisher and Tomy.
Players pit two characters from the Naruto manga and anime series against each other, using basic attacks and special techniques to defeat their opponent.
Mortal Kombat : Deception is a fighting game developed and published by Midway as the sixth installment of the Mortal Kombat series.
The game also introduces the 'Combo Breaker', a system which allows players to interrupt combos up to three times per match.
Marvel vs. Capcom 2 : New Age of Heroes is a crossover fighting game developed and published by Capcom.
Players select a team of characters from the Marvel and Capcom universes to engage in combat and attempt to knock out their opponents.

evidence: The Kaivalya Upanishad is an ancient Sanskrit text and one of the minor Upanishads of Hinduism.
It extols Shiva, aloneness and renunciation, describes the inner state of man in his personal spiritual journey detached from the world.
The text is notable for presenting Shaivism in Vedanta, discussing Atman (Soul, Self) and its relation to Brahman.
The Gospel According to John is one of the four canonical gospels in the New Testament.
The author is identified as 'the Disciple whom Jesus loved'.
The community appears to define itself primarily in contrast to Judaism, rather than as part of a wider Christian community.
The Tithonus poem is a poem by the archaic Greek poet Sappho.
The poem deals with the effects of ageing.
It has been partially known since 1922, but in 2004 more papyrus fragments were published, drawing international media attention.
Du Friedefürst, Herr Jesu Christ, BWV 116, is a church cantata written by Johann Sebastian Bach in 1724.
The cantata is based upon Jakob Ebert's hymn 'Du Friedefürst, Herr Jesu Christ'.
Bach wrote the cantata for the 25th Sunday after Trinity as part of his second annual cycle of mostly chorale cantatas.
Nyanyi Sunyi is a 1937 poetry collection by Amir Hamzah.
The collection consists of 24 titled poems and pieces of lyrical prose.
Analysis of Nyanyi Sunyi has focused on the theme of God and His relationship to humanity, as well as of aspects of human existence: fate, dissatisfaction, and escape.

evidence: Bachata # 1 's , Vol . 3 is a compilation album released by Machete Music on March 30 , 2010.

Upon release , the album peaked at number forty-one on the Billboard Latin Albums chart and number four on the Billboard Tropical Albums chart.
' Lay You Down ' is a song by American R & B recording artist Usher.
It was released to urban radio on September 28 , 2010 in the United States.
The song received positive reviews from critics , who praised its production and Usher 's vocals.
Rated R : Remixed is the second remix album by Barbadian singer Rihanna.
It was released on May 8 , 2010 , in Brazil and Europe.
The majority of the remixes were remastered to incorporate influences from the genre of house music.
' Lo Mejor de Mí ' is a song written and produced by Rudy Pérez and first recorded by Spanish singer Juan Ramon.
In 1997 , Mexican recording artist Cristian Castro covered the song for his fifth studio album Lo Mejor de Mí.
Castro 's version peaked at number-one on the Billboard Hot Latin Songs and the Billboard Latin Pop Songs charts in the United States.
' Hot Tottie ' is a song by recording artist Usher.
It was sent to rhythmic and urban airplay on August 9 , 2010.

evidence: Hilbert space = The mathematical concept of a Hilbert space, named after David Hilbert, generalizes the notion of Euclidean space.
Hilbert spaces arise naturally and frequently in mathematics and physics, typically as infinite-dimensional function spaces.
Hilbert spaces are indispensable tools in the theories of partial differential equations, quantum mechanics, Fourier analysis...
Integer sorting is the algorithmic problem of sorting a collection of data values by numeric keys, each of which is an integer.
Algorithms designed for integer sorting may also often be applied to sorting problems in which the keys are floating point numbers or text strings.
Integer sorting algorithms are usually designed to work in either the pointer machine or random access machine models of computing.
The impedance analogy is a method of representing a mechanical system by an analogous electrical system.
The impedance analogy preserves the analogy between electrical impedance and mechanical impedance.
The impedance analogy is widely used to model the behaviour of mechanical filters.
A very common use is in the field of audio equipment, such as loudspeakers.
An antimetric electrical network is an electrical network that exhibits anti-symmetrical electrical properties.
For an antimetric network, the two impedances must be the dual of each other with respect to some nominal impedance R0.
The conditions for symmetry and antimetry can be stated in terms of two-port parameters.
For a two-port network described by impedance parameters (z-parameters), if the network is symmetric, and if the network is antimetric.
The mobility analogy, also called admittance analogy or Firestone analogy, is a method of representing a mechanical system by an analogous electrical system.
The mobility analogy preserves the topology of the mechanical system when transferred to the electrical domain.
The mobility analogy is widely used to model the behaviour of mechanical filters.
Another very common use is in the field of audio equipment, such as loudspeakers.

Jim convinces Dwight that the building is unsafe, leading to Dwight renting a bus.
Jim tries to make Pam happy with some pie.

The season earned seven Primetime Emmy Award nominations.

Liz takes Pete Hornberger to a book signing to meet Rosemary Howard.
Liz goes back to Jack's office and begs for her job back.
The episode was praised by critics, with Alec Baldwin winning the Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Lead Actor.

The series depicts the everyday lives of office employees in the Scranton, Pennsylvania branch of the fictional Dunder Mifflin Paper Company.
Andy Bernard creates an incentive program.
He promises to tattoo himself if the staff earn a certain amounts of points.
'The Incentive' received mixed reviews from critics.

Chemical provided personal and commercial checking accounts, savings and time deposit accounts, personal loans, consumer financing and mortgage banking.

evidence: Delaware Route 404 ( DE 404 ) is a major state highway in Sussex County , Delaware that spans the width of the state.
DE 404 serves as a major route connecting the Chesapeake Bay Bridge and the Baltimore-Washington Metropolitan Area with the Delaware Beaches.
DE 404 begins at the Maryland border, where it continues northwest into that state as MD 404.
The route comes to an intersection with US 13 and turns south to form a concurrency with that route on Sussex Highway.
There have been seven business routes of US Highway 31 in the state of Michigan.
The two former routes, for Niles and Holland were a business spur and loop, respectively.
Business US Highway 31 ( Bus . US 31 ) was a business spur running to the Niles downtown area along a former routing of US 31.
Business US Highway 31 ( Bus . US 31 ) was a 4.1-mile-long ( 6.6 km ) business loop running through Holland.
Delaware Route 30 ( DE 30 ) is a state highway in Sussex County , Delaware.
DE 30 has an alternate route, DE 30 Alternate ( DE 30 Alt . ) , that was created by 1999 to connect the route with DE 1.
DE 30 begins at an intersection with Bi-State Boulevard in southwestern Sussex County between Laurel and Delmar.
The route turns southeast before it curves to the east again.
Delaware Route 4 ( DE 4 ) is a state highway in New Castle County , Delaware.
The route passes through suburban areas in northern New Castle County between Newark and Wilmington.
DE 4 begins at an intersection with Elkton Road in Newark.
The road runs through wooded areas and comes to a bridge over Amtrak 's Northeast Corridor railroad line.
Delaware Route 71 ( DE 71 ) is a state highway in New Castle County , Delaware.
The route loops off U.S. Route 13 ( US 13 ) from south of Townsend to Tybouts Corner.
DE 71 heads to the northwest from US 13 on two-lane undivided Summit Bridge Road.
The road passes to the east of Townsend and intersects Pine Tree Road / Main Street.

evidence: Mycena fonticola is a species of fungus in the Mycenaceae family.
It is known only from central Honshu, in Japan, where it grows on dead leaves and twigs in low-elevation forests dominated by oak trees.
The fungus was first collected by Japanese mycologist Haruki Takahashi in 1999, and described as a new species along with seven other Japanese Mycenas in a 2007 publication.
Mycena clariviolacea is a mushroom in the Mycenaceae family.
It is known only from Kanagawa, Japan, where it fruits on dead fallen twigs in forests dominated by oak and chinquapin trees.
The mushroom was first collected by Japanese mycologist Haruki Takahashi in 2000, and, along with seven other Mycena species, was reported as a new species in a 2007 publication.
Mycena multiplicata is a species of mushroom in the Mycenaceae family.
The mushroom is known only from Kanagawa, Japan, where it grows on dead fallen twigs in lowland forests dominated by oak.
The mushroom was first collected by Japanese mycologist Haruki Takahashi in 1999, and reported as a new species in a 2007, along with seven other Japanese Mycenas.
Mycena lanuginosa is a species of mushroom in the Mycenaceae family.
It is known only from lowland oak-dominated forests in central Honshu in Japan.
Mycena lanuginosa was first collected by Haruki Takahashi in 2000, and published as a new species in 2007, along with seven other Japanese Mycena species.
Mycena intersecta is a species of mushroom in the Mycenaceae family.
It is known only from central Honshu, in Japan, where it is found growing solitarily or scattered, on dead leaves in lowland forests dominated by oak.
The mushroom was first collected by Japanese mycologist Haruki Takahashi in 1999, and published as a new species (along with seven other Japanese Mycenas) in a 2007 publication.

evidence: The Battle of Krasos was a battle in the Arab – Byzantine Wars that took place in August 804
Nikephoros set out to meet them but was surprised at Krasos and heavily defeated
Nikephoros ' accession in 802 resulted in a resumption of warfare between Byzantium and the Abbasid Caliphate
Despite his defeat, and a massive Abbasid invasion the next year, Nikephoros persevered
Sa'd al-Dawla Abu 'l-Ma'ali Sharif, more commonly known by his laqab (honorific epithet), Sa'd al-Dawla
he inherited the throne at a young age and in the midst of a major Byzantine offensive
Sa'd came to rely increasingly on Byzantine assistance
the state his father had left him was crumbling: the Byzantine emperor Nikephoros II had just conquered Cilicia
Géza II was King of Hungary and Croatia from 1141 to 1162
he waged wars against the Byzantine Empire on behalf of his allies
Géza intervened at least six times in the fights for Kiev on behalf of Iziaslav II of Kiev
he could not prevent the Byzantines from restoring their suzerainty over them
Andrew III the Venetian was King of Hungary and Croatia between 1290 and 1301
Hungary was in a state of constant anarchy during Andrew's reign
the leading Croatian and Slavonian lords were opposed to his rule
Andrew expelled the adventurer from Hungary and forced Albert of Austria to conclude a peace

evidence: NSB Class 64 is a class of three electric multiple units built by Strømmens Værksted for the Norwegian State Railways.
They were built for the opening of the Hardanger Line and served there until 1985.
The Hardanger Line opened in 1935 as a steep and curvy branch of the Bergen Line to connect Bergen to the Hardangerfjord.
NSB Di 3 is a class of 35 diesel-electric locomotives built by NOHAB for the Norwegian State Railways (NSB).
The class was built between 1954 and 1969, and delivered in two series, Di 3a and Di 3b.
It was ordered as part of the company 's dieselization of services during the 1950s and 1960s.
NSB Class 66 was a three-car electric train used by the Norwegian State Railways for express trains on the Østfold Line to Halden and Gothenburg.
The class was the electric counterpart of diesel Class 88.
One of the Class 66 trains was involved in the Hjuksebø train disaster in 1950.
T2000 was an electric train formerly used on the Oslo Metro of Oslo, Norway.
Each was 18 metres long, and could carry 185 passengers.
The T2000 operated on Line 1 of the T-bane, and replaced the aging HkB 600 teak wagons used on the Holmenkoll Line.
The NSB El 17 is a class of twelve electric locomotives built by Thyssen-Henschel and Norsk Elektrisk & Brown Boveri for the Norwegian State Railways.
The traction system of the El 17 was based on the DB Class 120 of Germany.
The unreliability and lack of sufficient power forced NSB to instead use them in the regional Vestfold and Gjøvik Lines.

evidence: The 1989 – 90 South-West Indian Ocean cyclone season was an average cyclone season
The season officially ran from November 1, 1989, to April 30, 1990.
The first storm, Cyclone Alibera, was the second longest-lasting tropical cyclone on record
Cyclone Gregoara was the strongest of the season.
The 1980 Atlantic hurricane season was tied with 1932, 1969, and 1994 for most named storms
The season officially began on June 1, 1980, and lasted until November 30, 1980.
Hurricane Allen was then the earliest Category 5 hurricane on record
Tropical Storm Hermine caused significant flooding in Mexico.
The 1992 – 93 South-West Indian Ocean cyclone season lasted longer than normal
It was an above-average season, with four tropical cyclones.
Cyclone Colina formed and struck Réunion
Cyclone Edwina was the longest-lasting and strongest storm of the season.
Hurricane Florence was a strong, late season hurricane
With peak winds of 110 mph (175 km/h) and a minimum pressure of 972 mbar.
Florence developed out of an area of low pressure associated with a stalled frontal system
The storm intensified into a subtropical storm shortly thereafter.
The 1960 Atlantic hurricane season was the least active season since 1952
The season officially began on June 15, and lasted until November 15.
The most significant storm of the season was Hurricane Donna
Collectively, the tropical cyclones in 1960 caused at least 250 deaths.

evidence: The Constitution of the Republic of Singapore Tribunal was established in 1994 pursuant to Article 100 of the Constitution of the Republic of Singapore.
Questions referred to the Tribunal may concern the validity of enacted laws or of bills that have not yet been passed by Parliament.
Parliament attempts to circumvent or curtail the discretionary powers conferred on the President by the Constitution.
If the Tribunal rules that the proposed amendment does have the effect of restricting the discretionary powers of the President...

The Fifteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution prohibits the federal and state governments from denying a citizen the right to vote based on that citizen's race, color, or previous condition of servitude.
To enforce the amendment, Congress enacted the Voting Rights Act of 1965.
In the final years of the American Civil War and the Reconstruction Era that followed, Congress repeatedly debated the rights of the millions of black former slaves.
By 1869, amendments had been passed to abolish slavery and provide citizenship and equal protection under the laws.
United States v. More was a United States Supreme Court case in which the Court held that it had no jurisdiction to hear appeals from criminal cases in the circuit courts by writs of error.
More ensured that the Court's criminal jurisprudence would be limited to writs of error from the state.

Relying on the Exceptions Clause, More held that Congress's enumerated grants of appellate jurisdiction to the Court operated as an exercise of Congress's power to eliminate all other forms of appellate jurisdiction.
An election for the Speaker of the U.S. House of Representatives took place on October 29, 2015, during the 114th U.S. Congress.
Due to friction within the Republican Party caucus, Boehner decided to step down as speaker and resign his seat in Congress.
The Speaker of the United States House of Representatives is the presiding officer of the United States House of Representatives.
The 435 members of the House of Representatives elect a Speaker by majority rule at the beginning of each session.
The doctrine of legitimate expectation in Singapore protects both procedural and substantive rights.
To derogate from the representation may amount to an abuse of power or unfairness.

The recognition of substantive legitimate expectations is somewhat controversial as it requires a balancing of the requirements of fairness against the reasons for any change in the authority's policy.
The procedural dimension of the doctrine of legitimate expectation has been recognized by Singapore courts.
